Title: Xpeng launches new X9 with RM279,888 starting price
Article:
BERMAX Xpeng Sdn Bhd has introduced the new Xpeng X9, a flagship seven-seat electric multi-purpose vehicle (MPV) designed to combine luxury, advanced technology and intelligent mobility.
The new X9 starts from RM279,888 and is positioned as Xpeng's latest offering for families seeking a premium, connected and future-ready driving experience.
Bermaz Auto Bhd group chief executive officer Datuk Seri Francis Lee said the launch reflects Xpeng's ambition to make advanced mobility technologies more accessible while strengthening Malaysia's electric vehicle (EV) ecosystem.
"Xpeng's vision as the Explorer of Future Mobility is anchored in making cutting-edge technology accessible to all, supported by its AI Tech Tree Strategy that unites AI, energy solutions and embodied intelligence," he said.
He added that Xpeng's move towards establishing completely knocked down (CKD) operations at the EP Manufacturing Bhd facility in Melaka would help enhance local capabilities and support Malaysia's transition into a more competitive EV landscape.
The new X9 retains its futuristic design with Xpeng's signature "Robot Face" front fascia, seamless daytime running lights and a streamlined silhouette.
New upgrades include power front soft-close doors, adding convenience and refinement to everyday use.
Inside, the cabin features a refreshed interior palette with options including Coffee, Meteorite Black and a new Rose Brown dual-tone design.
The MPV also comes with a 21-inch head-up display, ultra-fiber steering wheel and upgraded 27-speaker X-Opera audio system.
Designed for families, the X9 offers a flexible second-row 40/60 split-folding configuration, heated third-row seats and a smart refrigerator with child-lock protection.
It is built on Xpeng's 800V silicon carbide high-voltage platform with 5C super-charging capability.
The Standard Range variant uses a 94.8kWh lithium iron phosphate (LFP) battery, offering up to 535km range and charging from 10-80 per cent 12 minutes.
The Long Range and all-wheel-drive Performance variants feature a 110kWh nickel cobalt manganese (NCM) battery, delivering up to 615km and 580km of range respectively, with peak charging power of up to 542kW.
Safety and intelligent driving are supported by XPENG's XPILOT Assist system powered by the Turing AI chipset, which delivers up to 750 trillion operations per second of computing capability.
The system supports advanced driver assistance features including adaptive cruise control, lane-centering, lane-change assistance, automated parking, remote parking and a 360-degree camera system.
The fully imported X9 is available in five exterior colours, including Midnight Black, Arctic White, Matte Gray, Lambent Cyan and Polar Violet.
The model comes with a five-year manufacturer warranty or 120,000km, five years of free service and maintenance or 100,000km, and an eight-year high-voltage battery warranty up to 160,000km.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Trump: Peace deal with Iran in final stages
Article:
WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump on Tuesday said negotiations over a peace deal to end the Middle East war were in their final stages, after Iran and Israel halted attacks that threatened to reignite the months-long conflict.
Iran fired missiles at Israel on Sunday in response to strikes against Lebanon's Hizbollah in the southern suburbs of Beirut, before Israel struck back despite Trump's efforts to dissuade Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu from doing so.
The Israeli leader said of Iran that the "fire on that front is contained" hours after Teheran said it had stopped its military action, while both sides warned they could resume hostilities despite diplomatic efforts gathering pace.
Iran and Israel "were going back and forth and now they both agreed through me to stop and we're in the final throes of what will be a very, very good deal," the US leader told reporters on his return from an NBA Finals game.
When asked whether a deal would be a matter of days or weeks, he said it would take "two or three days."
The flare-up came after weeks of negotiations seeking to bring about a definitive end to the regional war sparked by US and Israeli strikes on Iran on Feb 28, with Teheran insisting a halt to the conflict must include a truce in Lebanon.
Trump, who has reportedly grown increasingly exasperated with Netanyahu, had earlier urged both sides to stop "shooting" and said that "final negotiations" towards peace would proceed "subject to ignorance or stupidity getting in its way."
US Vice President JD Vance told Fox News on Monday that while the United States and Israel shared interests, their positions did not always align.
"The Israelis and the United States, we have a lot of shared interests," Vance said. "But we also have some situations where our interests diverge."
- LEBANON CITY EVACUATES -
In the recent escalation of attacks, Iran fired nearly 30 missiles at Israel, according to the Israeli military, while Israel struck military sites in the Islamic republic.
Iranian state media reported on Tuesday that two members of the "Army Air Defence Force" were killed in Israeli strikes a day earlier. No casualties were reported in Israel following the exchange.
And despite Iranian demands, Israel has kept up its attacks on Lebanon, where it is fighting the Iran-backed group Hizbollah.
On Tuesday, Lebanese state media reported an Israeli strike on the southern city of Tyre after the Israeli military urged residents to evacuate, the first time it had done so for the entire city and its surrounding areas.
"Enemy warplanes launched a heavy strike on Tyre," the state-run National News Agency said.
The news sent residents of Tyre including its Christian quarter fleeing for safety, an AFP correspondent said, reporting heavy traffic heading north.
"For your safety, we ask you to immediately evacuate your homes... and move north of the Zahrani River," the Israeli military's Arabic-language spokesman Avichay Adraee said on X.
Last week, Israel's military alleged that Hizbollah members were operating in the Christian quarter and said it would warn people to leave if the group remained there.
Lebanon was drawn into the war when Hizbollah fired rockets at Israel on March 2 to avenge the US-Israeli killing of Iran's supreme leader.
Israel responded with an extensive campaign of airstrikes and a ground invasion that have killed nearly 3,600 people. Exchanges of fire with Hizbollah have not stopped despite an ongoing truce.
The heads of the Pakistani and Lebanese armed forces meanwhile agreed to boost cooperation on Tuesday as they met in Pakistan with peace talks over the Middle East war dragging on.
On another front of the war, Israel's military said early Tuesday it had intercepted an aerial target from Yemen, but there were no injuries.
- OIL EASES -
Fears had grown that the recent flare-up would put at risk a fragile Middle East truce – in place since April 8 – and ruin efforts for talks to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, through which about a fifth of global oil usually passes.
The conflict has severely disrupted shipping through the Hormuz strait, while Washington has imposed a blockade on Iranian ports.
Trump said Tuesday that two pilots were uninjured after reports that a US military helicopter went down near the Strait of Hormuz.
It was not immediately clear whether the Apache helicopter was shot down, experienced mechanical failure or encountered some other problem, the New York Times reported, citing two people briefed on the incident.
In better news for Iranians, local media reported early Tuesday that Teheran's international airport – closed during the missile exchanges – had reopened, allowing flights carrying haj pilgrims from Saudi Arabia to land.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt steps up El Nino preparedness as hot, dry weather expected until 2027
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The government, through the National Disaster Management Agency (Nadma), is strengthening the country's level of preparedness following forecasts of more challenging hot and dry weather conditions due to the El Nino phenomenon, which is expected to persist until early 2027.
This includes cross-agency cooperation to address any disaster risks arising from the prolonged hot and dry conditions.
Nadma director-general Meor Ismail Meor Akim said that although the situation remains under control, the government is not taking it lightly and has instructed all relevant agencies to enhance their preparedness and implement mitigation measures early to ensure public safety, the continuity of water supply and the nation's well-being.
"Three cloud-seeding operations have been scheduled to be carried out in the near future to help increase rainfall distribution and reservoir levels in affected areas.
"The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) will continue to monitor the situation and issue updates on the El Nino phenomenon throughout the Southwest Monsoon period," he said in a statement today.
MetMalaysia has also been instructed to issue haze forecasts and provide forest fire prediction data generated through the Fire Danger Rating System (FDRS), with projections covering the next seven days, to the Fire and Rescue Department .
Meor Ismail said the fire department has been directed to work with the Department of Environment to continuously monitor 83 hotspots identified as being at high risk of open burning throughout the Southwest Monsoon period as part of early mitigation efforts.
"The Department of Irrigation and Drainage must identify dams requiring maintenance so that the relevant parties can take immediate action, while the Department of Mineral and Geoscience (JMG) must identify additional groundwater source locations for the construction of tube wells as a mitigation measure in water-stressed areas," he said.
On public health, he said the Health Ministry had been tasked with intensifying public awareness campaigns on the health effects of hot weather, particularly among high-risk groups.
At the same time, the Education Ministry has been asked to roll out a nationwide awareness programme on coping with hot weather for school students to enhance awareness and reduce the risks associated with the Southwest Monsoon.
He said the whole-of-government approach remains a key pillar of the Madani Government's efforts to ensure that all relevant agencies maintain an optimum level of preparedness to address any disaster risks arising from hot and dry weather conditions.
"Nadma will continue to monitor developments and coordinate the actions of all relevant agencies to ensure that the safety, well-being and interests of the people remain the top priority," he said.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Duopharma subsidiary secures RM155.3mil Health Ministry insulin supply contract
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Duopharma Biotech Bhd's wholly-owned subsidiary, Duopharma (M) Sdn Bhd (DMSB), has secured a RM155.28 million contract from the Health Ministry to supply recombinant human insulin products and reusable insulin pens to government healthcare facilities.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, the pharmaceutical company said DMSB had received and accepted a letter of award (LOA) from the government for the three-year contract, which commenced on June 3, 2026 and will expire on June 2, 2029.
"The contract is in the ordinary course of business of the Duopharma Biotech group of companies and will enable DMSB to supply the products to Health Ministry's facilities.
"With an estimated 4.75 million diabetics in Malaysia, of whom about 450,000 patients are treated with recombinant human insulin in government hospitals and clinics, DMSB hopes to improve diabetes management in the country by providing high-quality and cost-effective biosimilar insulins," it said.
Under the contract, DMSB is required to provide an irrevocable performance bond of RM2.59 million within 30 days of accepting the LOA, Duopharma said.
On risk factors, the company said these include execution risks related to storage, transportation and delivery logistics, changes in the economic, political and regulatory environment, as well as operational risks such as shortages of raw materials and skilled labour.
It added that none of the company's directors, major shareholders or persons connected to them has any direct or indirect interest in the contract.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Dayang Enterprise forms JV with Brunei partner to expand oil and gas presence
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Dayang Enterprise Holdings Bhd's (DEHB) wholly-owned subsidiary, Dayang Enterprise Sdn Bhd (DESB), entered into a joint venture (JV) and shareholders agreement with Brunei-based Petrokon Utama Sdn Bhd (PUSB) on May 27 for a proposed project collaboration in Brunei.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, DEHB said that the proposed JV would enable the group to strengthen its strategic presence in Brunei and enhance its capability to participate in maintenance, turnaround and construction activities through collaboration with an established local partner.
"The collaboration is also expected to complement the DEHB Group's existing expertise and support its long-term growth strategy within the regional oil and gas industry," it said.
Under the agreement, DESB and PUSB will incorporate a joint venture company in Brunei, to be known as Petrokon Dayang Corporation Sdn Bhd or such other name as may be approved by the relevant authorities.
It said the JV partners will each hold a 50 per cent equity stake, with an initial paid-up capital of 100,000 Brunei dollars.
"DESB shall be entitled to appoint up to two directors to the board of the joint venture company, and one of DESB's nominee directors shall be appointed chairman of the board.
"The JV agreement shall become effective upon fulfilment of the conditions precedent, including, amongst others, the incorporation of the joint venture company and the award of the project in Brunei," it added.
The company said the agreement is not expected to have any material effect on its issued share capital, substantial shareholders' shareholdings, earnings per share, net assets per share or gearing for the financial year ending Dec 31, 2026.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit closes higher against greenback, regional peers on easing Middle East tensions
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit closed higher against the US dollar and was mostly stronger against regional currencies on Tuesday, supported by improved market sentiment amid easing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.
At 6pm, the local note appreciated to 4.0580/0630 against the US dollar from Monday's close of 4.0715/0760.
SPI Asset Management managing partner Stephen Innes said that broader regional risk appetite has improved following the tentative truce between Iran and Israel.
"The truce has resulted in easing in oil prices, which helped set a more constructive tone across asset classes, with Brent crude oil retracing part of its previous gains and easing pressure on regional risk assets," he told Bernama.
According to media reports on Monday, Iran announced that it would halt its attacks for the time being but warned that it would resume attacks if Israel engaged in further acts of aggression, including military actions in Lebanon.
Iran and Israel have halted their overnight attacks, marking the first direct exchange of strikes since a ceasefire took effect in early April.
At the time of writing, Brent crude oil declined by 1.93 per cent to US$92.43 per barrel.
Meanwhile, Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said regional currencies also strengthened against the US dollar, buoyed by easing tensions in the Middle East and supported by stronger-than-expected economic data from China.
"China's trade in May also surprised on the upside with total exports growing 19.4 per cent year-on-year versus consensus estimates of 15.0 per cent, suggesting that global demand remains positive despite higher fuel prices.
"Additionally, the United States Consumer Price Index (CPI) for May, which will be released on June 10  (US time), will be closely monitored ahead of the Federal Open Market Committee's (FOMC) meeting next week," he said.
The ringgit traded mostly lower against a basket of major currencies.
It rose against the Japanese yen to 2.5332/5365 from 2.5445/5475 at Monday's close, but eased versus the British pound to 5.4329/4395 from 5.4249/4309 yesterday, and turned slightly lower vis-a-vis the euro at 4.6915/6972 from 4.6867/6919 previously.
However, the local currency mostly strengthened against regional peers.
It gained versus the Singapore dollar to 3.1563/1604 from 3.1577/1614 on Monday, and was up against the Thai baht at 12.3531/3732 from 12.3942/4128 previously.
It strengthened against the Philippine peso to 6.59/6.60 from 6.60/6.61 yesterday, but it was slightly lower against the Indonesian rupiah to 224.7/225.1 versus 223.8/224.2 previously.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MARKET PULSE PM JUNE 9, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia continued its downward trend on Tuesday, ending the session at 1,675 points.
The index was dragged lower by banking and telco stocks, as investors turned cautious ahead of the release of the US May consumer price index data.
The ringgit ended slightly stronger against the US dollar, closing at 4.0620.
In the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin eased to around RM255,251, while Ethereum also traded lower at the RM6,801 level.
That wraps up today's Market Pulse.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Parliament must take lead on child rights, says Dewan Rakyat speaker
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul said Parliament has a unique role in advancing children's rights, stressing that it requires coordinated action, political will and laws that deliver practical impact.
"Parliament's role goes beyond passing laws to include scrutiny, accountability and fostering national dialogue," he said at the Symposium on Children's Rights Legislative Reform in Parliament.
One of the strongest messages emerging from the symposium is that protecting children is a shared responsibility, he said.
He said the role of protecting them cannot rest solely with one ministry, one agency or one level of government.
Johari urged stronger cooperation across institutions and between federal and state authorities, calling for meaningful partnerships with civil society, communities and families.
"Most importantly, it requires political will as lawmakers must ensure legislation reflects the best interests of children and addresses pressing issues such as child protection, access to education, documentation, digital safety and social inclusion.
"Our responsibility extends beyond policy intention to the practical impact of the laws we enact," he said.
He added that the insights and recommendations from the event should not remain confined to conference proceedings but should inform future parliamentary debates and legislative reforms.
"The true measure of success for this symposium will not be the quality of the discussions alone, but the actions that follow."
Johari said a nation's future is ultimately shaped by how it treats its children.
"When we invest in their rights, welfare and development, we invest in the future strength, stability and prosperity of our country."
Also present at the event were Bukit Bendera MP Syerleena Abdul Rashid, Ampang MP Rodziah Ismail and Suhakam commissioner Dr Farah Nini Dusuki.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man arrested video clip of hit-and-run involving cyclist goes viral
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested the driver of an orange Perodua Myvi involved in a road crash accident that left a young cyclist injured in Kampung Pandan here this morning.
Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the incident occurred at 7.26am along Jalan Wirawati 5 and involved a 45-year-old man driving the car and a boy riding a bicycle.
"Initial investigations found that the accident happened when the driver was turning right out of a junction before colliding with the rear of the bicycle, causing the cyclist to fall onto the road.
"The driver is believed to have left the scene without stopping following the collision," he said in a statement today.
Zamzuri said investigators later tracked down the driver.
"Checks revealed that the man was driving a vehicle belonging to his sibling and is a holder of a Person with Disabilities card under the mental disability category. He possesses a valid driving licence.
"Police have yet to determine the extent of injuries suffered by the cyclist, whose identity has not been established," he said.
Zamzuri urged the victim's family members to come forward and lodge a police report to facilitate investigations.
He also called on witnesses, including the owner of any dashcam footage of the incident, to contact investigating officer Inspector Hazman at 019-2089005 or visit the nearest police station.
The case is being investigated under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for reckless and dangerous driving.
Anyone found guilty is liable to up to five years' jail and a fine of between RM5,000 and RM15,000 for a first offence, and up to 10 years' imprisonment and fine of between RM10,000 and RM20,000 for subsequent offences.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: New hansard system to improve access to parliamentary records
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Parliament has introduced a new Hansard management system aimed at improving the efficiency of parliamentary administration through a more integrated approach to recording and accessing official debate records.
Digital Minister Gobind Singh Deo said the strategic initiative, implemented through GovTech Malaysia, was designed to provide faster and more systematic access to key parliamentary documents while reducing reliance on physical copies.
"With the implementation of this system, access to Hansard records will become easier, faster and more systematic, helping to enhance transparency, efficiency and accessibility of information for stakeholders and the public," he said at the handover ceremony of the Parliament Hansard project at the Parliament building today, which was also attended by Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Dr Johari Abdul and Dewan Negara Deputy President Datuk Nur Jazlan Mohamed.
Gobind said the Digital Ministry, through its whole-of-government approach, would continue to spearhead the country's digital transformation efforts to ensure government service delivery remains focused on the needs of the people.
"It is time for us to provide digital platforms that enable seamless interactions for all parties, with our ultimate goal being to ensure that no one is left behind in a trusted digital environment," he said.
Meanwhile, the Digital Ministry, in a statement, said that the new system transforms the traditional PDF-based Hansard format into a smarter digital platform.
The system allows users to conduct keyword searches across multiple parliamentary sittings simultaneously, view debate trends through a word cloud feature and filter search results by members of parliament.
"The system serves as a digital platform to facilitate searches and analysis, but it does not replace the official Hansard documents published by Parliament. All data is based on official sources to ensure the accuracy and integrity of information," the statement read.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Wild black bear in Japan captured after multi-day hunt captures the nation's attention
Article:
UTSUNOMIYA: The Japanese city of Utsunomiya captured a wild black bear on Tuesday after a dramatic multi-day search that gripped the nation, as local schools closed and residents were urged to stay indoors.
The city closed all 94 municipal primary and middle schools for a second straight day on Tuesday after its first-ever bear sighting on Saturday evening.
Authorities decided to keep schools closed again on Wednesday due to a report of a possible second bear roaming the city, an official said.
Bear attacks have spiked in Japan, including in urban areas, prompting the government to set up a task force this year to reduce incidents.
In fiscal 2025, the country reported a record 238 casualties, including 13 deaths, according to the environment ministry.
With about 500,000 residents, Utsunomiya, in Tochigi Prefecture, is part of the Greater Tokyo Metropolitan region, about 100 km (60 miles) north of the capital.
When the bear resurfaced in a residential area early on Tuesday afternoon, police cars and other vehicles involved in the search promptly blocked off the vicinity.
For more than an hour, police officers milled about, with some holding long sticks and others metal shields, as some national broadcasters aired live footage filmed from helicopters.
The adult bear, which was estimated to weigh about 100 kg (220 lbs), was eventually shot with a tranquiliser gun, loaded onto a cage on a truck and driven away.
The city has yet to decide what to do with it, an official said.
Around 100 km to the northeast, Iwaki, in Fukushima Prefecture, also suspended classes at three schools on Tuesday in a neighbourhood where a black bear was spotted a day earlier.
Last week, a bear attack in Fukushima city left at least four people injured, with security footage in one incident showing the animal chasing a man and throwing him to the ground.
Asiatic black bears are listed as a vulnerable species globally, but their numbers are estimated to have tripled in Japan since 2012, aided by a decline in hunting.
Experts say climate change has reduced harvests of natural bear food like acorns and beechnuts, while the depopulation of rural areas and the proliferation of abandoned farmland have emboldened them to seek nourishment near human settlements.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man arrested for insulting, obstructing traffic cop after summons
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested a 25-year-old man for allegedly hurling insults at a traffic policeman and obstructing a public servant from carrying out his duties following a traffic enforcement operation in Sentul.
Sentul police chief Assistant Commissioner Basri Sagoni said the incident occurred at about 8.24am on June 8 when a policeman conducting traffic enforcement duties issued a summons to the owner of a Proton Prevé for a traffic offence.
He said the vehicle owner allegedly approached the officer, recorded a video using his mobile phone and uttered abusive remarks while provoking the policeman out of dissatisfaction with the summons issued.
"Following a police report and investigations, a team from the Sentul district police headquarters arrested the suspect at about 2.36am today at a business premises in Jalan Dato Senu, Kuala Lumpur.
"A mobile phone and SIM card believed to be linked to the case were also seized," he said in a statement today.
He said initial investigations found that the suspect admitted recording the policeman because he was unhappy with the enforcement action taken against him.
The case is being investigated under Section 186 of the Penal Code for obstructing a public servant in the discharge of public functions and Section 14 of the Minor Offences Act 1955.
He said the suspect has been remanded for three days from today until June 11 to facilitate investigations, adding that a urine screening test conducted on him returned negative results.
Basri reminded the public not to speculate or spread information that could interfere with police investigations.
Any form of insult, threat, harassment or action that obstructs public servants from carrying out their duties is an offence under the law, and firm action would be taken against offenders, he said.
The incident gained attention after a video of the confrontation went viral on social media.
The footage shows a uniformed traffic policeman speaking to an individual beside a vehicle during what appears to be a roadside stop.
In the video, he is seen remaining calm while explaining the enforcement action, with the person recording the encounter repeatedly engaging him in a verbal exchange and demanding the policeman's identification number.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pas may leave or be expelled from PN after split with Bersatu - Analysts
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pas may either leave or be expelled from the Perikatan Nasional (PN) coalition, after deciding to end its political cooperation with Bersatu, according to a political analyst.
Director of the Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities at Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM) Kuala Lumpur Campus, Associate Professor Dr Mazlan Ali, said Pas could not determine PN's direction on its own, as the coalition also comprises Bersatu, Gerakan and the Malaysian Indian People's Party (MIPP).
"Pas represents only one voice. Since Pas has severed ties with Bersatu, there is a possibility that the three component parties in PN could decide to expel Pas, and the coalition is expected to be led by Bersatu president, Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin," he told Bernama.
He said Pas may form a new political alignment to face the next general election, further fragmenting the opposition bloc.
"We will likely see two different opposition blocs ahead of the 16th General Election (GE16) - PN, led by Bersatu, and another new alignment, spearheaded by Pas," he said.
Mazlan said the split between Pas and Bersatu could potentially benefit PH and BN, if Malay support is no longer concentrated within a single opposition coalition.
Yesterday, Pas president, Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang, announced that the party had officially decided to end its political cooperation with Bersatu, after taking into account reports, studies and current assessments of relations between the two parties.
Following the decision, Muhyiddin, in a statement today, said that Bersatu, as a founding party of PN, would remain in the coalition and continue to strengthen its core struggle.
Meanwhile, Professor Datuk Dr Sivamurugan Pandian, of the Centre for Policy Research and International Studies at Universiti Sains Malaysia, said Pas and Bersatu were expected to face political repercussions following the decision, as well as significant challenges in winning voter support.
He said Bersatu was likely to be more affected, as it relied heavily on Pas' grassroots machinery and support base.
"However, Pas will also face challenges in expanding its influence in mixed constituencies, without the support of a strategic partner," he said.
Sivamurugan said the development had also created uncertainty within the opposition bloc, and opened the door for other parties to realign their strategies, to strengthen their positions ahead of GE16.
– BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fahmi: 4G coverage to hit 100pc, 5G at 98pc by 2030
Article:
SHAH ALAM: The government is targeting full 4G coverage and 98 per cent 5G coverage in populated areas by 2030, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said.
Fahmi said current 5G coverage had reached about 84 per cent nationwide, while efforts were ongoing to further expand connectivity across the country.
"In the ministry and government's planning, the target set by 2030 is 100 per cent 4G coverage in populated areas.
"Meanwhile, 5G coverage is expected to reach 98 per cent by 2030," he told reporters after a working visit to the CelcomDigi Advanced Intelligent Warehouse here today.
Fahmi said 5G technology was increasingly important for industries as it allowed more devices, equipment and machines to communicate simultaneously compared with 4G and WiFi.
He said this was particularly useful in warehouses, factories and industrial areas that depended on Internet of Things (IoT) applications, real-time monitoring and automated systems.
He said 5G Standalone (5G SA) technology could also help realise the concept of a "data highway" by allowing large amounts of data to move rapidly between machines and systems in real time.
Unlike existing 5G networks that rely partly on 4G infrastructure, 5G SA operates on full 5G architecture, offering faster response times, lower latency and support for more connected devices.
Fahmi said the technology had also matured, with many solutions now more accessible, affordable and tested for use by small and medium enterprises (SMEs).
He said the CelcomDigi warehouse, which measures 10,000 sq ft, could provide storage capacity that would previously have required a 40,000 sq ft facility.
He said this showed that 5G-based automation could help businesses reduce space requirements while improving efficiency, monitoring and product movement.
Fahmi said he had also asked the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission to engage telecommunications companies on expanding 5G applications, particularly for SMEs.
He said the government was also studying whether incentives could be proposed to the Finance Ministry to encourage wider adoption of the technology.
"We want SMEs to see for themselves how 5G technology can benefit their operations.
"At this time, we are drafting several proposals to be brought into the 2027 Budget. I hope this can be finalised soon, possibly within one to two weeks," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cautious sentiment drags Bursa Malaysia lower at the close
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended lower on Tuesday as investors adopted a cautious stance amid external uncertainties, keeping a close watch on the upcoming United States inflation as well as developments surrounding the fragile Israel-Iran ceasefire.
At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) finished 4.02 points lower at 1,675.50 compared with Monday's close of 1,679.52.
The FBM KLCI opened 8.41 points higher at 1,687.93 earlier and traded between 1,675.04 and 1,687.93 throughout the session.
Losers were slightly ahead of gainers in the broader market by 570 to 533, while 548 counters were unchanged, 1,024 untraded and 13 suspended.
Turnover narrowed to 3.76 billion units worth RM2.90 billion from 3.48 billion units worth RM2.92 billion yesterday.
Berjaya Research Sdn Bhd head of research Kenneth Leong said the FBM KLCI edged lower on Tuesday, reversing all of its intraday gains and bucking the recovery trend seen across most regional peers, dragged down by selling pressure across glove and telecom heavyweights.
"Looking ahead, the FBM KLCI is attempting to stabilise after recent bouts of volatility, though the path to a sustainable recovery remains tentative.
"Near-term direction will be heavily influenced by external cues, with investors keeping a close watch on the upcoming US inflation print slated to be released tomorrow night, which is a key data point that could meaningfully shift market expectations around the Federal Reserve's rate trajectory," he told Bernama.
Leong also said that looking from a technical aspect, the key index has formed a bearish candlestick, implying a persistent selling pressure with the immediate support located at 1,672 points and thereafter at 1,665 points.
"Meanwhile, the near-term resistance remains pegged at the 1,700 psychological level, followed by 1,707 points," he added.
IPPFA Sdn Bhd director of investment strategy and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan added that renewed Pentagon scrutiny of several major Chinese companies had also influenced market sentiment.
"Market attention remains firmly focused on the US inflation, with a firmer inflation print reinforcing the higher-for-longer interest rate narrative and temper global risk appetite.
"While elevated oil prices are likely to keep headline inflation above four per cent in the near term, we expect inflationary pressures to gradually moderate towards year-end and into 2027, potentially providing a more supportive backdrop for risk assets," he said.
Among the heavyweights, Maybank declined by eight sen to RM10.62, Public Bank down one sen to RM4.77, Tenaga Nasional erased six sen to RM14.14, CIMB was two sen easier at RM7.37 but IHH Healthcare earned 10 sen to RM8.70.
Among the active stocks, Hong Seng Consolidated inched up half a sen to 1.5 sen, Zetrix AI accumulated half a sen to 82.5 sen, TFP Solutions improved three sen to 8.5 sen but Tanco lost 32 sen to 80 sen.
As for the top gainers, Nestle surged RM2.44 to RM94.44, Malaysian Pacific Industries added RM1.30 to RM46.80, Ajinomoto advanced RM1.06 to RM15.48, Concrete Engineering jumped 91 sen to RM4.90, and Vitrox was 42 sen better at RM7.16.
Among the top losers, FACB was 19 sen lower at RM1.66, Petronas Gas lost 18 sen to RM17.32 and Sunway was 12 sen easier at RM5.13.
On the index board, the FBM Emas Index fell 18.12 points to 12,451.96, the FBM Top 100 Index notched down 23.50 points to 12,291.99, and the FBM Emas Shariah Index declined 7.66 points to 12,435.38.
The FBM Mid 70 Index dropped 8.62 points to 18,001.77, while the FBM ACE Index edged up 79.30 points to 4,727.68.
By sector, the Financial Services Index shrank 63.60 points to 19,496.88, the Industrial Products and Services Index eased 0.33 of a point to 196.68, and the Energy Index slid 3.31 points to 787.63, but the Plantation Index gained 17.04 points to 8,742.44.
Main Market volume expanded to 2.22 billion units valued at RM2.57 billion from 1.68 billion units valued at RM2.53 billion yesterday.
Warrants declined rose to 997.45 million units valued at RM155.37 million from 1.25 billion units valued at RM188.07 million previously.
ACE Market volume improved to 550.14 million worth RM171.11 million from 545.18 million units valued at RM194.35 million.
Consumer products and services counters accounted for 178.92 million shares traded on the Main Market, followed by industrial products and services (323.70 million), construction (98.31 million), technology (974.91 million), financial services (82.85 million), property (181.73 million), plantation (29.76 million), real estate investment trusts (16.12 million), closed-end funds (48,900), energy (105.01 million), healthcare (120.62 million), telecommunications and media (33.30 million), transportation and logistics (32.40 million), utilities (37.62 million), and business trusts (23,000).

Title: Malaysia keeps fiscal discipline amid subsidy rise, inflation pressures remain contained
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government is maintaining fiscal discipline and adjusting its spending plans to manage rising global price pressures, even as fuel subsidy costs rose to RM7.5 billion in April.
Finance Minister II Amir Hamzah Azizan said the flexible approach has enabled the government to manage expenditure more effectively while encouraging Malaysians to play their part in addressing current challenges, including the impact of geopolitical tensions and global supply disruptions.
He said inflation remained moderate at 1.9 per cent as of end-March, within the government's target range of 1.5 per cent to 2.5 per cent, and compared favourably with many other countries.
Despite cost pressures stemming from global supply chain disruptions, he said the inflation rate has remained under control, supported by earlier reforms and targeted measures introduced by the government, he added.
Amir Hamzah said some sectors, particularly small and medium enterprises (SMEs), are more vulnerable to rising costs.
However, the government has opted against implementing a large-scale economic stimulus package.
"Stimulus measures could fuel inflation further, with the impact eventually being felt by the wider population," he said during the "Balancing Fiscal Focus with External Risks" session at the Invest Malaysia Kuala Lumpur Conference 2026.
Amir Hamzah said the government has introduced targeted support measures for affected businesses and individuals.
This includes RM5 billion through banking institutions, RM5 billion under financing guarantee schemes and a further RM5 billion through micro-financing programmes.
Banking institutions have also been encouraged to offer loan restructuring, repayment moratoriums and other forms of assistance to customers facing financial difficulties.
Amir Hamzah said fuel subsidy expenditure posed a significant fiscal challenge, rising from around RM700 million per month in January and February to RM5 billion in March before peaking at RM7.5 billion in April.
However, he noted that with oil prices moderating, fuel subsidy costs are now estimated at between RM3.5 billion and RM4 billion.
To ease fiscal pressures, the government has introduced cost-saving measures across ministries and agencies. Ministries have so far committed to generating around RM5 billion in savings through various initiatives, including work-from-home arrangements that help reduce operating costs.
He said the measures show that fiscal management requires a coordinated effort across the entire government.
"Previous reforms have created sufficient fiscal space for Malaysia to respond to external shocks.
"They enable the government to continue supporting economic growth, keep businesses operating, preserve jobs, shield the public from inflationary pressures and manage the country's fiscal position prudently.
"We are confident of navigating current challenges through a balanced and disciplined approach," he said.
On broader global uncertainties, Amir Hamzah said safeguarding the country's energy security remains a key priority amid ongoing conflict in the Middle East, alongside continued fiscal reforms aimed at strengthening public finances.
He said the government's first priority is ensuring adequate energy and fuel supplies, as any disruption could affect the wider economy.
Malaysia has not experienced supply shortages despite concerns over global energy markets, he added.
"We've been blessed because we're net energy exporters and we have our own capacity.
"Local energy institutions and companies are also actually quite well positioned on a global basis, and they have supply sources that they can actually activate on a global basis to kind of cushion the blow to us along the way. Securing supply was the greatest priority that we had to focus on," he added.
Amir Hamzah said the government, through Petronas and other oil companies, conducts weekly monitoring to ensure the country's energy supplies remain secure.
He said the government also assesses which sectors are most exposed to cost pressures in order to determine appropriate intervention measures while maintaining its commitment to protecting vulnerable groups from rising living costs.
He said crude oil prices have fallen from around US$120 per barrel previously to between US$90 and US$100 per barrel, helping to reduce subsidy costs.
As a result, the government has maintained current petrol prices and the RON95 subsidy quota to ensure consumers continue to benefit from stable fuel prices.
At the same time, access to RON95 subsidies has been tightened for ineligible groups to reduce leakages and ensure assistance reaches those who genuinely require support.
"This is important to ensure government resources are used more efficiently and that subsidies are directed towards those who need protection the most," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Italy probe into Israel's treatment of flotilla activists sparks spat
Article:
ROME: Italy on Tuesday condemned as "unacceptable" an insult by Israel's National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir, following a decision by prosecutors in Rome to investigate him over the treatment of activists in a Gaza-bound aid flotilla.
Ben Gvir is being investigated for an alleged "war crime" and "torture" after activists accused Israeli authorities of mistreatment during their detention last month, according to Italian media reports.
"The land of the boot has become the land of the flip-flop," Ben Gvir wrote Monday on X – a derogatory reference to the geographical shape of Italy.
Italian Foreign Minister and Deputy Prime Minister Antonio Tajani told a Senate hearing Tuesday they were "unacceptable words... unworthy of a minister", and which demonstrate "the man's political and moral standing."
"I have no words to comment on what he said about Italy yesterday, after learning that he was under investigation by the public prosecutor's office," Tajani said.
Israel detained more than 430 activists from countries around the world after intercepting them in international waters on May 18 as they made the latest in a string of attempts to break the Israeli blockade of the Palestinian territory.
Ben Gvir sparked widespread condemnation after he posted a video mocking the flotilla activists while their hands were bound.
Tajani said Rome "will continue to push" for the European Union to sanction Ben Gvir, adding that "many countries" were in favour of doing so.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three arrested over viral video clips of wheelie stunts in Kuala Lumpur
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have arrested three motorcyclists and a pillion rider believed to be involved in separate viral "wheelie" incidents across several roads here following intelligence-led operations.
Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the arrests were made between 12.30am and 1.30am today at the suspects' respective homes.
"In the first case, police acted on a 47-second video uploaded on Facebook Reel by "jenterapejuang.my", showing two motorcycles — a Honda EX5 and a Yamaha 135LC FI — performing wheelie stunts along Jalan Raja Laut, Jalan Tuanku Abdul Rahman and several other locations in Kuala Lumpur.
"The incident was believed to have taken place between 2am and 2.30am on June 6," he said in a statement.
He added that following investigations, police arrested two men aged between 17 and 24, as well as a 17-year-old male who was riding pillion.
All three are being investigated under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for reckless and dangerous riding that endangered both themselves and other road users.
In a separate case, Zamzuri said police investigated another 45-second viral video uploaded on Facebook Reel by "UPDATE&INFO", which showed a Honda EX5 rider performing a wheelie before crashing along Jalan Ampang, Jalan Raja Laut and several other roads in Kuala Lumpur.
"The incident was believed to have occurred between 2am and 2.30am on June 7.
Police subsequently arrested an 18-year-old male believed to be the rider involved," he said adding that he is also being investigated under Section 42(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987.
Zamzuri reminded the public not to engage in illegal street-racing activities or dangerous stunts such as wheelies, warning that such behaviour posed serious risks to public safety.
Those with information related to the incidents have been urged to contact the Tun H.S. Lee traffic police station at 03-20719999 or the nearest police station.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: No revival of Muafakat Nasional, Zahid says
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has ruled out any possibility of reviving Muafakat Nasional (MN), a cooperation once formed between Umno and Pas.
When asked whether there was any possibility of it being revived, he said Umno had no intention of bringing MN back in any form, according to Buletin TV3.
"MN has been registered as an NGO by someone. So we have absolutely no intention and no desire to revive MN.
"Whether it is 1.0 or 2.0, that is over, and we have closed the book," he said when met by the media at the Invest Malaysia 2026 Conference here today.
The MN cooperation between Umno and Pas was formally sealed in 2019 when the federal government was led by Pakatan Harapan (PH).
However, the cooperation ended after Pas formed Perikatan Nasional (PN) together with Bersatu the following year.
Meanwhile, Zahid said Barisan Nasional (BN) would contest all 36 state assembly seats in the Negri Sembilan state election on its own.
He said the decision was made after PH announced it would contest all 36 seats.
"Yes, in Negri Sembilan BN will definitely go
solo
because PH has announced they are contesting all 36 seats.
"This means they will field candidates in all state constituencies. Therefore, we have no choice but to contest solo," he said.
In the August 2023 state election, BN won 14 seats while Perikatan Nasional won five seats. PH controlled 17 seats through DAP's 11 seats, PKR's five seats and Amanah's one seat.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sharper premium electric Zeekr X 2026 bookings open in Malaysia
Article:
ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Malaysia has opened bookings for the 2026 Zeekr X, an updated premium urban electric SUV.
The X features stronger performance, upgraded technology and enhanced comfort features as Zeekr expands its presence in Malaysia's EV market.
The refreshed Zeekr X arrives with upgrades across both the Premium and Flagship variants, offering improved performance, enhanced comfort, and new styling elements under the theme "Boldly Yours".
The latest model introduces several new standard features including automatic front doors, a fridge, an intelligent interactive B-pillar, a multifunction steering wheel with touch bar controls and a larger 404-litre boot.
The X Flagship AWD now produces 365kW and 573Nm, enabling a 0-100km/h sprint in just 3.7 seconds.
It also gains faster 22kW onboard charging, a 445km WLTP range, massage and ventilated front seats, a black interior with red seatbelts, new 20-inch black forged wheels and exclusive colours including Onyx Black and Matte Khaki Green.
The Premium RWD variant delivers 250kW and 373Nm, with an 11kW onboard charger, 415km WLTP range, a dual-tone interior, new 19-inch wheels and a heads-up display.
It also introduces the Porcelain Pink exterior colour.
Safety remains a key focus, with the X equipped with seven airbags and an ADAS suite supported by five HD cameras, five millimetre-wave radars and 12 ultrasonic sensors.
"With enhanced performance, meaningful feature upgrades, and a more refined design expression, the 2026 Zeekr X is designed to meet the expectations of drivers seeking an electric vehicle that combines innovation, style and everyday usability," said Eddy Lu, general manager of Zeekr Malaysia.
The 2026 Zeekr X is expected to be priced between RM160,000 and RM180,000.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BN to go solo in Negri Sembilan election
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Barisan Nasional will contest solo in the Negri Sembilan state election, said its chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
"Yes, in Negri Sembilan BN will definitely go solo because Pakatan Harapan (PH) has announced that it will contest all 36 state assembly seats.
"This means we have no choice but to contest solo," he said when met by reporters after delivering the keynote address at the Invest Malaysia Kuala Lumpur 2026 conference here today.
The state legislative assembly was dissolved on June 4 after receiving the consent of the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, at an audience held at Istana Besar Seri Menanti on the same day.
Negri Sembilan is the
second state to dissolve
its assembly to pave the way for the state election, after Johor did so earlier this month.
Meanwhile, DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke recently said the party, together with Pakatan Harapan (PH), would contest all 36 state seats in the state.
The decision was made in response to BN Johor's move to contest all 56 state seats in the Johor state election.
Zahid said BN would continue its cooperation with the unity government until the end of the current term.
"We are still working closely as a team and I think we should continue (the cooperation) at least until the end of the term," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Rival leaders of Turkiye's main opposition party call competing meetings
Article:
ANKARA: The arch-rival leaders of Turkiye's main opposition - one of them elected and the other controversially court-appointed - both announced plans to address party lawmakers at competing meetings on Tuesday, in a stand-off that could deepen a crisis among President Tayyip Erdogan's challengers.
A Turkish court last month annulled the Republican People's Party's (CHP) 2023 congress that elected Ozgur Ozel as chairman, citing irregularities.
It also reinstated to the post Kemal Kilicdaroglu, the party's divisive former leader who lost to Erdogan in the 2023 presidential election, in a move that hit financial markets and which critics described as politicised.
Both men had previously said they would address the weekly CHP meeting in parliament. MPs backing Ozel began gathering in the parliamentary hall several hours before it was due to begin at 1030 GMT.
Kilicdaroglu then announced a new plan to hold a meeting at party headquarters, also in the capital Ankara.
"I am calling on all our party members, and every citizen whose heart beats for this country, to join us," Kilicdaroglu said in an X post, setting a time of 1100 GMT for his meeting.
[[nid:1459111]]
The opposition's challenges could boost Erdogan's prospects of extending his more than two-decade rule of Nato-member Turkiye in elections scheduled to be held by 2028, but which analysts say could come earlier if the government seeks to take advantage of the CHP strife.
Kilicdaroglu's return and recent criticism of the party have enraged his detractors.
The meeting could mark one of Ozel and his elected team's last efforts to maintain control of the CHP, the party of modern Turkiye's founder Mustafa Kemal Ataturk.
The secular and centrist CHP, running roughly even with Erdogan's Islamic-rooted and conservative ruling AK Party (AKP) in polls, has also faced an unprecedented judicial crackdown since 2024 in which hundreds of members and elected officials have been detained as part of corruption charges that the party denies.
Kilicdaroglu has said he would purge the party of corruption, referring to the cases involving CHP-run municipalities.
The ousted leadership denies the allegations and calls them a politically motivated and anti-democratic "coup" - a charge the government rejects, saying courts are independent.
The CHP has 138 deputies in the 600-seat assembly and around two-thirds of them voted after the court ruling to make Ozel head of its parliamentary group.
Cavit Soydas, a CHP voter in the northeast village of Tekke, said on Sunday that Ozel "should fight through every possible legal means" to hang on to the party but if that fails "we are ready to go under the name of another party."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Don't blame us to cover up your weaknesses, Bersatu man tells Pas
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A Bersatu member has hit back at Pas Youth chief Afnan Hamimi Taib Azamudden, rejecting claims that the party is obstructing unity among the Muslim community.
Zulkifli Ibrahim also accused Afnan of misrepresenting decisions made within Perikatan Nasional.
The former Sungai Acheh assemblyman said the allegation that Bersatu was prioritising group interests over broader political cooperation was "unfounded" and inconsistent with the official stance of the opposition coalition leadership.
He said such remarks risked confusing Pas and Bersatu members as well as PN supporters, who expect leaders to rely on formal decisions rather than political perception.
He pointed to a statement by PN secretary-general Datuk Seri Takiyuddin Hassan, who is also Pas secretary-general, on May 16, announcing that the coalition had agreed in principle to an electoral pact with selected parties sharing common political interests.
He said the position was reaffirmed in an official media statement on May 17, and was not a personal view of any individual leader.
"Why is Bersatu being blamed when the decision to open cooperation space was announced by the Pas secretary-general himself on behalf of PN?"
Zulkifli also questioned whether Afnan was disagreeing with the coalition's agreed position or attempting to contradict official statements issued by Pas and PN leadership.
He suggested the remarks could be an attempt to divert attention from internal party decisions and shifting positions within Pas.
"Bersatu has never obstructed any genuine effort towards unity that strengthens the opposition and benefits the people," he added, stressing that the party remained committed to PN as a platform for political cooperation based on mutual principles.
Zulkifli said unity could not be built on accusations, but required consistency and honesty among leaders.
"If Pas wants to speak about unity of the ummah, it must first demonstrate unity in its own statements and actions," he said.
Zulkifli added that the public was increasingly able to distinguish between facts and political narratives, and would not be swayed by inconsistent messaging.
"Speaking the truth is the first condition for unity," he said.
Yesterday, Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang announced the
termination of political cooperation
with Bersatu following a special meeting of the party's central working committee.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 2027 Budget challenge: Managing fiscal deficits while empowering the rakyat
Article:
LETTERS:
Even with greater uncertainty and volatility in the global economic environment, with the disruption in the global supply chain, plus the energy crisis, our economy still managed to grow at a high and commendable rate of 5.4 per cent in the first quarter of this year.
Inflation rate is still stable, and the unemployment rate remains low. Investors' confidence to the economy is solid, while the ringgit is performing well vis-a-vis its regional peers.
But the impact of the war in the Middle East to the economy has forced the government to balance a very delicate act.
On the one hand, there is a need to rationalise expenditure further, but not to the extent of tampering the growth momentum, and on the other, the need to maintain fuel subsidy and spend more on social assistance to those in need, but not in a way that can worsen the fiscal deficit further and to hike inflation in the near future.
It is about finding the sweet spot of the management of the economy while embracing the uncertainty of the ongoing developments of the crisis.
Prime Minister, Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has hinted that there will be more cost-saving measures in the upcoming 2027 Budget.
This is to be expected, as the cost of maintaining fuel subsidy, especially for RON95 and diesel are getting more difficult to sustain.
Thus, in the second half of this year and moving on to 2027, there is a clear need for the government to rationalise its spending to redirect it to the people at large.
In the past budgets, when it comes to the cost of living issue, the focus was more on reducing cost and expenditure. Thus, it is time for the government to increase incomes holistically.
Anwar, in his book, "SCRIPT For a Better Malaysia: An Empowering Vision and Policy Framework for Action", where it has become the basis for Malaysia Madani framework, has highlighted a few important suggestions about raising incomes.
The crucial one is "to introduce legislation to establish a living wage that provides the means of basic economic survival for all".
The book also emphasised that "we need to make sure the people's economic well-being is sustainable as well. We will need to explore the concepts of minimum wage, liveable wage, and universal basic income to determine the best and most practical legal implementation in order to alleviate poverty".
If these ideas and suggestions being detailed out further in the upcoming 2027 Budget, especially the idea of a universal basic income, that would be the real game changer that people are yearning for.
The last four Madani budgets have shown the courage to reform.
Perhaps the fifth would make Malaysians really feel the impact of the good macroeconomic figures and enjoy what the humane economy is really about.
DR IRWAN SHAH ZAINAL ABIDIN
Senior Associate Fellow
Economic and Financial Policy Institute
Universiti Utara Malaysia
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: ASNB enhances zakat fulfilment via new al-mustaghallat, khultah method
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Muslim unitholders of Amanah Saham Nasional Bhd (ASNB) fixed-price funds can now opt for the "zakat al-mustaghallat" via "khultah" (collective) payment method.
In a statement today, ASNB said that through the khultah method, unitholders can receive net income distributions after a 2.57 per cent zakat deduction, calculated only on the distributed income, not on the investment principal.
It said that to enable ASNB to manage the zakat obligations of Muslim unitholders, effective May 25, 2026, all fixed-price funds have been restructured into a multi-class format comprising class A and class B.
It said all existing unitholders of fixed price funds are automatically placed under class A.
"There are no changes to their investment or the way income distributions are received, and unitholders will continue to receive full income distributions, without any zakat deduction.
"Class B is open to Muslim unitholders who wish to manage their zakat more conveniently, where income distributions are received after zakat deduction," it said.
ASNB said unitholders who prefer to manage their zakat independently may choose to remain in class A, while opting for class B enables unitholders to benefit from a comprehensive zakat management approach while maintaining competitive net returns, thereby supporting long-term investment sustainability.c
"The zakat collected will be channelled to the respective state zakat agencies for distribution to eligible 'asnaf' (group), in accordance with the rulings of the relevant zakat authorities.
"The zakat rate is set at 2.57 per cent for all ASNB fixed-price funds, namely ASB, ASB 2, ASB 3 Didik, Amanah Saham Malaysia (ASM), ASM 2 Wawasan and ASM 3, in line with the Masihi (or Gregorian) calendar approach and international zakat accounting standards," said ASNB.
Meanwhile, Permodalan Nasional Bhd acting president and group chief executive Datuk Rizal Rickman Ramli said the introduction of this new collective zakat al-mustaghallat payment method forms part of PNB's ongoing commitment to strengthening the socio-economic well-being of Muslim unitholders.
"ASNB provides zakat payment options that enable unitholders to fulfil their obligations conveniently and systematically, in line with shariah requirements," he said.
He also expressed appreciation to the relevant national and state religious authorities for their consideration of the zakat al-mustaghallat calculation method via khultah.
"This method supports a more systematic zakat distribution framework and helps unitholders achieve their long-term investment goals with competitive net returns," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Loke on alleged PN-Umno letter leak: Let Negri Sembilan voters decide
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke has brushed aside allegations of a Bersatu-Pas-Umno attempt to topple caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun before the state assembly was dissolved last week.
He said the people should be the ones to determine the future of the Negri Sembilan administration in the upcoming state election.
"We have dissolved the state assembly. Lets wait for the voters to asses the situation and make a decision.
"What was exposed, Bersatu and Pas' plans, the people will evaluate everything," Loke told reporters after launching the Cermat Madani programme here today.
His remarks were in response to allegations of attempts by Bersatu, Pas and Umno to topple caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun before the state assembly was dissolved.
Loke said Negri Sembilan voters can see what had occurred in the state, adding that any battle would take place fairly in the state elections.
In the last election, Pakatan Harapan won 20 out of the 36 seats in the state assembly.
The media previously reported that a 13-page letter allegedly from Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin to Pas leaders suggested that Perikatan Nasional
held prior discussions
with Umno on efforts to unseat Aminuddin, before the 14 Umno assemblymen withdrew support for Aminuddin's administration.
The alleged letter was dated May 25.
The Negri Sembilan state assembly was
dissolved on June 5
, paving the way for the 16th state election.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Fisherman hauls up human skull in net
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: A human skull, believed to belong to a man aged between 20 and 30, was discovered in waters off Sedili after it became entangled in a fishing net on Sunday.
District police chief Superintendent Yusof Othman said police were alerted to the discovery at 8.10pm.
The skull was caught in a fishing net by a 44-year-old boat skipper while he was fishing in Sedili waters at 3am the day earlier.
Yusof said a forensic examination at Kota Tinggi Hospital determined that the skull is believed to belong to an adult male aged between 20 and 30.
"The case has been classified as a sudden death pending further investigation and identification of the deceased," he said in a statement today.
Those with missing family members or information that could assist the investigation can contact the Kuala Sedili police station chief Inspector Mohammad Aizat Ahmad at 018-246 4667.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt reviewing 2027 development plans from ministries: Akmal Nasrullah
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government is reviewing development proposals submitted by ministries for 2027 as it moves ahead with the 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP).
Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the ministry is reviewing the proposed projects and programmes before further assessments begin next month.
It will ensure development allocations are channelled towards initiatives that support economic growth and improve public well-being.
"All ministries have submitted their development planning proposals for 2027,"he told reporters on the sidelines of the Invest Malaysia Kuala Lumpur Conference 2026.
"At the ministry, we continue to view matters from a development perspective. While we acknowledge that 2026 is a year in which we need to manage the economy amid various challenges, preparations for 2027 must continue," he added.
Akmal said project prioritisation would take into account economic conditions, fiscal considerations and the country's broader development needs.
The government, he said, remains focused on delivering the 13MP, Malaysia's five-year development blueprint for 2026-2030, despite a more challenging economic environment.
Akmal said 2026 has been designated as a year of execution while planning for 2027 will reflect current economic realities.
"We have given a commitment that 2026 is about execution. At the same time, planning for 2027 will take into account the realities and challenges that we are facing today," he said.
He added that the government would use the MyRMK platform to monitor the progress of projects and initiatives under the five-year plan.
Capital allocations would continue to prioritise innovation-driven and high-growth sectors, he said.
Earlier during a session titled "Navigating External Uncertainties, Strengthening Resilience", Akmal said Malaysia's established semiconductor industry provides a strong foundation for future growth, with the country aiming to move further up the value chain.
"When we talk about packaging and testing, we are already there in terms of expertise. But we plan to uplift this further and continue moving up the value chain," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Late Genting heiress cut daughters' RM10 million cash bequests after feeling betrayed'
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The High Court was told today by lawyer Datuk Low Beng Choo that the late Genting heiress Lim Siew Kim had felt angry and betrayed by her two daughters and had initially intended to exclude them entirely from her RM1.6 billion estate.
Low testified that Siew Kim, the third child of late Genting Group of Companies founder Tan Sri Lim Goh Tong, had originally bequeathed RM10 million in cash to her two daughters in her first will executed on Nov 2, 2021 before later deciding to substantially reduce their inheritances.
In the second will dated April 11, 2022, Lim reduced the cash bequests for her second and third daughters, Chan T'shiao Li, 48, and Kimberly Chan Tshiao Miin, 45, to RM900,000 and RM100,000, respectively.
This prompted the siblings to contest their late mother's will involving assets valued at more than RM1.6 billion.
"She (Siew Kim) said the first plaintiff and her husband were stealing and making false claims from the companies that they were involved in and she felt very betrayed and upset," said Low.
"She (first plaintiff) was told to leave the company but eventually, she resigned on her own accord.
"As for the second plaintiff, she had made allegations about the state of mind of the mother (Siew Kim) while the second incident was when Siew Kim's husband was in the hospital, and they were engaged in an argument as to whether life support should be continued.
"However, we discussed the matter and at the end of the day, I told her they were still her daughters. She decided to give the amounts that were reduced.
"Siew Kim had remarked that the siblings should count themselves fortunate to receive any money at all."
Low was testifying as a defendant's witness in cross examination by lawyer Tan Sri Cecil Abraham.
She said she revisited the issue and had asked Siew Kim if she wished to alter the reduced bequests.
"She gave me an angry, very definite 'No, no change. They do not deserve it. They are lucky I am providing some'." she said.
T'shiao Li, who was listening attentively from the public gallery, was seen turning to a female acquaintance and speaking to her as Low made the revelation.
Describing her meeting with Siew Kim on April 11, 2022, Low said the deceased was in bed but appeared to be her usual self, alert and fully capable of answering questions.
"We engaged in discussions and casual conversation. For me, there was no reason to believe otherwise. She was lucid and she knew exactly what she wanted to do.
"In fact, this time, she was very specific about the bequest. She knew who and what she wanted to give. There was no reason to doubt otherwise," she said.
Low said Siew Kim's firm instructions led to her to incorporate the revised bequest into the third and prevailing will dated April 28, 2022. Siew Kim died of ovarian cancer in July 2022 at 73.
Low, who had acted as Siew Kim's lawyer for years, had refrained from elaborating on the reasons behind the changes to the bequests, leaving it to the court to determine whether she could disclose the information without breaching privilege.
To this, judge Mahazan Mat Taib said the court needed to know the reasons.
At one point during Low's testimony, lawyer Datuk Seri V. Sithambaram, representing the sisters, stood and objected arguing that the evidence being presented amounted to hearsay.
Apart from Low, the first and second defendants named in the suit are Malcolm Fernandez and Chan Mei Yee — the executors and trustees named in Siew Kim's will dated April 28, 2022.
The third defendant is the sisters' younger brother, Marcus Chan Jau Chwen, 42.
Marcus, who is Siew Kim's only son, was granted 30 per cent of the residuary estate along with authority over all her safe deposit boxes.
Siew Kim's eldest daughter, Cressa Chan T'shiao Yunn, was left RM10 million and property in Ampang Hilir, Kuala Lumpur.
Cressa's daughter, would receive RM50,000 a month. Abraham is representing Malcolm.
The trial continues on July 27.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Najib posts Onn Hafiz's picture with Chinese community, draws over 2,000 likes
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak has weighed in on Johor Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi's "No DAP" stance.
In a Facebook post, Najib shared a post from BN Johor Facebook page that shows an image of Onn Hafiz interacting with Chinese residents.
He described the photograph as "beautiful" and ended his post with a message of unity.
"Through unity, we remain together. We are one people, one nation and one shared goal," he posted.
At the Johor BN election machinery launch on June 7, caretaker menteri besar Onn Hafiz said he would rather be
out of power
than "sit at the same table" with DAP, stressing that his
stance was not driven by race or emotion
.
His remarks drew criticism from some quarters, prompting BN secretary-general Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir to defend the stance as a matter of political principle rather than racial sentiment.
In his post, Najib also shared a Johor BN message stating that Johor's strength lies not in politics that divide communities or rhetoric that pits one race against another, but in the belief that the state belongs to all its people.
"Not politics that divides. Not incitement that seeks to make one race fear another race. But the spirit that Johor is our shared home," the post read.
Najib, the country's sixth prime minister, was also Umno and BN chairman from 2009 to 2018.
In 2022, he began serving a 12-year prison sentence after being convicted in the SRC International corruption case.
His sentence was later reduced to six years by the Pardons Board.
Although incarcerated, he remains a significant influence within Umno and BN politics.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bersatu excos can stay in Kedah govt if they back PAS-led administration, says MB
Article:
ALOR SETAR, June 9 — Bersatu state executive councillors can remain in the Kedah government despite PAS ending political cooperation with the party.
Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor said the only criterion was that they continue to support the PAS-led state government, news portal
Malaysiakini r
eported today.
“They stay, no changes as long as they continue to support the state government,” he was quoted as saying.
Sanusi, who is also PAS election director, was asked about the fate of Bersatu assemblymen in his administration following the breakdown in ties between the two Perikatan Nasional partners.
PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang announced the decision to halt cooperation with Bersatu last night after a special central committee meeting in Kuala Lumpur.
Hadi cited recent sharp clashes between the PN allies as the reason for the split.
Asked whether retaining Bersatu excos contradicted the party's stand, Sanusi said it did not.
“It's fine, our decision does not disrupt state governments – as long as they keep supporting, they can remain,” he was quoted as saying.
Kedah has two Bersatu excos, Datuk Halimaton Shaadiah Saad (Bukit Kayu Hitam) who oversees welfare, women, family and community, and Datuk Mohd Salleh Saidin (Kubang Rotan) who handles tourism, culture and entrepreneurship.
Sanusi previously also retained Suka Menanti assemblyman Dzowahir Ab Ghani, who holds the agriculture, plantation and transport portfolio, even after he was sacked from Bersatu for attending an event involving former deputy president Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin.

Title: Zahid: BN to go solo in Negeri Sembilan after Pakatan lays claim to all 36 seats
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 9 — Barisan Nasional (BN) will contest the upcoming Negeri Sembilan state election solo, said its chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.
The Umno president said BN had no choice but to go solo after Pakatan Harapan (PH) announced its intention to contest all 36 state seats.
“Yes, in Negeri Sembilan, we (BN) will definitely move solo because (PH) has announced that it will contest all 36 seats… So we have no choice but to contest on our own,” he told reporters after officiating the Invest Malaysia 2026 programme here today.
Ahmad Zahid, who is also Deputy Prime Minister, expressed optimism about BN’s performance and stressed that the coalition’s immediate priority was to secure a mandate from voters first.
On a broader note regarding the current federal leadership, he said the cooperative relationship within the government remains solid and is expected to continue at least until the end of the administrative term.
Ahmad Zahid described the synergy within the existing team as very strong, with all parties working closely together.
“We are very strong and working closely together as a team. I believe we should continue at least until the end of the term,” he said.
However, when asked about the exact date marking the end of the term, Ahmad Zahid gestured that the media should direct the question to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
Touching on the status of Muafakat Nasional, Ahmad Zahid stressed that speculation or efforts to revive the pact were no longer relevant and that the matter should be considered settled.
He said this was because the name had already been registered as a non-governmental organisation (NGO) by another party.
“Muafakat Nasional has already been registered as an NGO by another party. As such, we have no intention whatsoever of reviving Muafakat Nasional, whether version 1.0 or 2.0. The matter is final, and we have closed the chapter on it,” he said. — Bernama

Title: Anwar: Malaysia ready for next industrial leap with Japan in AI, digital and energy sectors
Article:
TOKYO, June 9 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged Japanese companies to continue expanding investments in Malaysia, particularly in high-technology sectors such as semiconductors, artificial intelligence, quantum computing and digital industries.
He said Malaysia has evolved from an economy based on rubber and tin to one driven by oil and gas, and is now strengthening its position as a regional hub for the electrical and electronics and semiconductor industries.
“We have become a semiconductor hub in the region, and that sector is expanding very rapidly. We have seen very large exports from the E&E sector.
“And I believe many of you, perhaps seven or eight companies, are also involved in the field of crypto computing,” he said during an introductory session with 29 selected Japanese business leaders here today.
Anwar said Malaysia is prioritising emerging technologies such as AI, digital industries and quantum computing while continuing to support traditional sectors including oil and gas.
He also highlighted regional energy connectivity initiatives, including undersea cable projects linking Southeast Asia, as well as potential collaborations involving Sarawak Energy and Malaysia, adding that the country looks forward to leveraging Japan’s expertise.
The Prime Minister, who is also Finance Minister, expressed appreciation for the Japanese business community’s long-standing role in Malaysia’s economic development.
“Many of you are familiar with Malaysia, have been to Malaysia, and I come here to thank you, many captains of the Japanese industry, for your support,” he said.
He said Japanese firms were among the earliest investors in Malaysia after independence, helping drive its industrialisation.
Anwar also noted growing cooperation between Malaysian and Japanese universities and research institutions, including his visit to the University of Tokyo earlier today, whose Research Centre for Advanced Science and Technology is set to collaborate with the International Islamic University Malaysia on advanced research.
Earlier, he met executives from six Japanese companies, including IBM, Mitsubishi Corporation, ENEOS Corporation, Shizen Energy, DENSO Corporation and Marubeni Corporation, to discuss opportunities in energy, the halal industry, artificial intelligence and advanced computing.
